channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Transportation Sec. Duffy suggests changing air traffic rules and antiquated tech
item.description: U.S. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy said on Wednesday he is reconsidering rules that allowed air traffic control supervisors to reduce staffing at Washington’s Reagan National Airport before a fatal Army helicopter-plane collision that killed 67 people last week.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Google scraps its diversity hiring goals as it complies with Trump's new government contractor rules
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is scrapping some of its diversity hiring targets, joining a lengthening list of U.S. companies that have abandoned or scaled back their diversity, equity and inclusion programs.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China’s DeepSeek web version is raising security alarms. Here’s why
item.description: The website of the Chinese artificial intelligence company DeepSeek, whose chatbot became the most downloaded app in the United States, has computer code that could send some user login information to a Chinese state-owned telecommunications company that has been barred from operating in the United
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: DOGE team seeks access to other agency systems after getting Treasury data
item.description: A Treasury official says DOGE team members have “read-only” access to the data from its payment systems as part of an “operational efficiency assessment." The post DOGE team seeks access to other agency systems after getting Treasury data first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Elon Musk’s China ties make a bad situation worse
item.description: In the first weeks of Donald Trump’s second term in office, Elon Musk has been busy seizing control of instruments of government power, causing many to wonder who is really in charge. “Elon Musk is President,” The Atlantic’s Jonathan Lemire declared. “There has never been a private citizen like him.
item.dc:creator: Scott Nover, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia claims near 50% boost in AI storage speed
item.description: Nvidia is touting a near 50% improvement in storage read bandwidth thanks to intelligence in its Spectrum-X Ethernet networking equipment, according to the vendor’s technical blog post.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: EXL’s Insurance LLM transforms claims and underwriting
item.description: As insurance companies embrace generative AI (genAI) to address longstanding operational inefficiencies, they’re discovering that general-purpose large language models (LLMs) often fall short in solving their unique challenges.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: East Bay tech company eyes 1,000-plus global layoffs, office space cuts
item.description: A big East Bay tech company warned on Wednesday that it plans to slash well over 1,000 jobs even as it pushes into AI opportunities.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Why this cybersecurity startup wants to watermark everything
item.description: Cybersecurity startup EchoMark is releasing a new application programming interface (API) to allow for its novel digital watermarking tool to integrate with virtually any existing communications software.
item.dc:creator: Jared Keller, Fast Company

item.title: Smartphone Apps, Diabetes Management, and Serious Health Risks
item.description: US health officials warn patients using smartphone apps to manage diabetes about serious health risks if they miss notifications needed to control their blood sugar levels.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Perrone, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Kyndryl expands Palo Alto deal to offer managed SASE service
item.description: Kyndryl has expanded its alliance with Palo Alto Networks to add secure access service edge (SASE) services to its managed services offerings.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nintendo PlayStation's First Game Would Have Been a Space Shooter, Says Former Exec Shuhei Yoshida
item.description: Former PlayStation executive Shuhei Yoshida reveals the Nintendo PlayStation's first game would have been a space shooter inspired by Silpheed
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: AI and scientists unite to decipher old scrolls charred by the Vesuvius volcano
item.description: U.K. scientists say they have made a historic breakthrough by making the first image of the inside of a scroll carbonized by a volcanic eruption 2,000 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless And Pan Pylas, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Warren finds common ground with Trump over debanking concerns
item.description: Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) seems to have found common ground with President Trump and Republican lawmakers over “debanking” concerns, urging the president Tuesday to take action to prevent Americans from being improperly locked out of the financial system.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Fast fashion, laptops and toys are likely to cost more due to US tariffs on Chinese imports
item.description: A sweeping new U.S. tariff on products made in China is expected to increase the prices American consumers pay for a wide array of products, from the ultra-cheap apparel sold on online shopping platforms to toys and electronic devices such as computers and cellphones.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: A wedding necklace and Polish dumplings will be part of a mission to the International Space Station
item.description: What do a wedding necklace, Polish savory dumplings and a photo of Hungary’s first astronaut have in common.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How to minimize your digital footprint when you travel
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy offers tips to dramatically reduce the risk of having your information stolen while booking flights, cruises, vehicles and accommodations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China targets Apple’s business practices; maybe the company should adapt
item.description: They say great art reflects its time. When it comes to Apple’s business, just as the iMac and iPod matched the zeitgeist, the company must now develop its business to meet the complex demands of a divided, post-globalist world struggling with economic and climate insecurity.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Silicon Valley’s brilliant young techies help Musk, Trump penetrate government, drawing Democrats’ fury
item.description: Bay Area members of Congress blasted incursions by Elon Musk's team into government's sensitive data and systems.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Israeli spyware company confirms US government and friends are customers
item.description: Israeli spyware company Paragon says the US government is a customer, shortly after it was called out by WhatsApp for targeting journalists.
item.dc:creator: benedict.collins@futurenet.com (Benedict Collins), TechRadar

item.title: Panasonic may leave the TV business, and that would be a crying shame
item.description: Japanese electronics manufacturer Panasonic is considering a sale of its TV business, only a year after it re-entered the US market.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Databricks acquires BladeBridge to aid data warehouse migrations
item.description: Data lakehouse provider Databricks has acquired data platform modernization software provider BladeBridge for an undisclosed sum to help its new customers move from rival data warehouses, such as Teradata, Snowflake, Amazon Redshift, and Microsoft SQL Server, to its lakehouse built atop Databricks S
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A spine-zapping implant helped 3 people with a muscle-wasting disease walk better
item.description: Three people with a muscle-destroying disease destined to worsen got a little stronger when an implanted device zapped their spinal cord.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: El Ministerio de Sanidad integrará los sistemas digitales de las CCAA en el propio
item.description: El Ministerio de Sanidad ha adjudicado a Ayesa los servicios de integración de las Comunidades Autónomas (CCAA) en sus sistemas de información sanitarios, a través del Nodo Central de la Intranet Sanitaria, así como el soporte asociado para que todas las incidencias que puedan surgir durante el proc
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: H-1Bs under Trump: Lots of heat, little action
item.description: Widespread opposition from hardcore supporters of US President Donald Trump is unlikely to lead to major changes to the H-1B visa program, which brings tens of thousands of foreign IT workers into the country each year.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Qorgau: Kazakhstan’s smart policing revolution in traffic management
item.description: Traffic management is a growing challenge for cities worldwide, requiring a balance between enforcement, efficiency, and public trust. In Kazakhstan, the Qorgau system is redefining road safety through an innovative fusion of artificial intelligence (AI), computer vision, and mobile technology.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Robinhood halts Super Bowl betting contracts after CFTC request
item.description: Robinhood said on Tuesday it is rolling back the event contracts that would let users bet on the result of the Super Bowl clash this weekend, after the online brokerage received a request from the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC).
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Rust update fixes ‘forever’ compilation
item.description: The Rust Team has published a point release update, Rust 1.84.1, to address a few regressions in last month’s Rust 1.84 release, including fixing painfully slow compilation related to the next-generation trait solver.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Judge says Elon Musk's claims of harm from OpenAI are a 'stretch' but welcomes possible trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Elon Musk's lawyers faced off with OpenAI in court Tuesday as a federal judge weighed the billionaire's request for a court order that would block the ChatGPT maker from converting itself to a for-profit company. U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Automated builds, tests, and quality gates are key to software quality – report
item.description: When it comes to software quality, the top three practices cited for production readiness include automated building and deploying, automated tests, and automated quality gates for every merge request, according to the JetBrains Qodana State of Software Quality Report 2024.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google's rising holiday's season ad sales aren't enough to ease worries about AI letdown
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google's digital ad sales continued to grow at a healthy clip during the holiday season, but that wasn't enough to offset investors' worries about whether its big bet on artificial intelligence will be lucrative as once envisioned.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: How to easily transfer files and photos off a computer to your hard drive
item.description: It's not just about freeing up space on your computer, Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says. It's about protecting your digital memories and important documents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AMD patches microcode security holes after accidental early disclosure
item.description: AMD on Monday issued two patches for severe microcode security flaws, defects that AMD said “could lead to the loss of Secure Encrypted Virtualization (SEV) protection.” The bugs were inadvertently revealed by a partner last week.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google makes its appeal to overturn jury verdict branding the Play Store as illegal monopoly
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google went to appeals court Monday in an attempt to convince a three-judge panel to overturn a jury's verdict declaring its app store for Android smartphones as an illegal monopoly and block the penalties imposed by a federal judge to stop the misbehavior.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Canada's most populous province to pause retaliatory measures as US puts tariffs on hold
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Ontario, Canada’s most populous province, said Monday he will pause all retaliatory measures against the United States after news broke that the threat of tariffs has been put on hold for a month.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: CISA hires former DHS CIO into top cyber position
item.description: Karen Evans is returning to the Department of Homeland Security in a top cyber job as DHS responds to major hacks into U.S. critical infrastructure. The post CISA hires former DHS CIO into top cyber position first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Trump signs order to create US sovereign wealth fund, eyeing TikTok purchase
item.description: President Trump on Monday directed secretaries to begin a process to create an American sovereign wealth fund, suggesting it could partially own the popular app TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Thinking of moving off VMware? Gartner tallies cost of large-scale VMware migration
item.description: Broadcom’s $61 billion acquisition of VMware caused quite a stir in the technology industry and continues to prompt VMware customers to consider alternatives.
item.dc:creator: Network World
